title: Aria rotundifolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information about "Aria rotundifolia." However, I found a plant called Aria (also known as Mespilus or Rowan) but the correct botanical name is Sorbus aucuparia. Here's a paragraph about it:

Aria, or Sorbus aucuparia, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The health benefits of Sorbus aucuparia include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to cardiovascular health and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus aucuparia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sorbus aucuparia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which may be used to support cardiovascular health, immune function, and antioxidant defenses.
taxonomy: Aria rotundifolia is classified as a member of the flowering plant family. It belongs to the genus Aria within the Rosaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with white or pink flowers and red berries.
